current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Keep the gated K=128 EF=16 architecture fixed and continue the gated stabilization sweep by testing LR=4e-4 in proxy.
Keep the gated K=128 EF=16 architecture fixed and start a gated AUXK sweep by testing AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 in proxy.
Return to the incubating top-k family and run a proxy architecture comparison at K=128 under the current best gated training settings.

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: The gated K=128 EF=16 model has now regressed at both 3.2e-3 and 8e-4, so the remaining clean learning-rate check is whether a more conservative signum step size of 4e-4 recovers a smoother optimization path and improves proxy FVU relative to the failed lower-LR probe.

With the gated K=128 EF=16 architecture now worse across all tested learning-rate alternatives, the next clean stabilization axis is auxiliary support pressure: increasing AUXK_ALPHA from 0.03125 to 0.0625 may improve feature health and residual coverage enough to recover proxy FVU without changing the core architecture or optimization regime.

Five non-improving rounds on gated-specific tweaks suggest the current search direction is locally exhausted, so the next meaningful move is to revisit a fundamentally different sparse selection mechanism. A clean top-k mainline comparison at K=128 using the current strongest training regime may show whether gated-specific routing is the bottleneck rather than the optimizer or regularization settings.

topk.design_hypothesis: Five non-improving rounds on gated-specific tweaks suggest the current search direction is locally exhausted, so the next meaningful move is to revisit a fundamentally different sparse selection mechanism. A clean top-k mainline comparison at K=128 using the current strongest training regime may show whether gated-specific routing is the bottleneck rather than the optimizer or regularization settings.
next_steps: If top-k proxy FVU improves materially over its earlier result and is competitive with gated, promote it to full and begin top-k stabilization from this stronger anchor.
If top-k remains clearly worse even under the stronger regime, conclude that a different architectural direction is needed and retire top-k from near-term focus.
If top-k is competitive on quality but notably better on runtime or stability, keep it alive as an alternate mainline family for subsequent cost-quality tradeoff work.

routed.design_hypothesis: Recent gated LR sweeps regressed, which suggests the quality ceiling is now architectural rather than a simple optimizer bracket. A new routed SAE family that separates top-k selection scores from decoded magnitudes, while warm-starting the support branch from the magnitude encoder, may preserve useful medium-strength features that plain gated currently suppresses too early and thus reduce FVU at K=128.
next_steps: If routed proxy FVU beats gated proxy materially, promote it and treat routed-specific stabilization as the new main quality path.
If routed quality is competitive but throughput regresses, keep the family alive and follow with a performance-focused implementation pass rather than discarding it on speed alone.
If routed is clearly worse, conclude that gated-family architectural refinement needs a different mechanism and try a different new family instead of more LR-only sweeps.

recent_performance_findings: round 3: suspected performance regression for jumprelu (tps=16601.650675, baseline_ratio=0.200000, termination=throughput_too_low)
next_move_guidance: If top-k proxy FVU improves materially over its earlier result and is competitive with gated, promote it to full and begin top-k stabilization from this stronger anchor.
If top-k remains clearly worse even under the stronger regime, conclude that a different architectural direction is needed and retire top-k from near-term focus.
If top-k is competitive on quality but notably better on runtime or stability, keep it alive as an alternate mainline family for subsequent cost-quality tradeoff work.
